                      FinServ context: If an attacker injects malicious Node.js control variables into an AI agent's environment, any child processes spawned during automated financial workflows could execute unintended or harmful commands.
                      First seen: 17 June 2026
                      CI/CD Workflow (Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery)
                      An automated software development pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys code changes whenever developers submit new code to a shared repository.
                      FinServ context: A compromised CI/CD pipeline at a financial services firm could allow an attacker to inject malicious code into a production application before it is reviewed, creating a software supply chain risk.
                      First seen: 24 June 2026
                      Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager
                      A centralised software platform used to manage and monitor Cisco's software-defined wide-area network infrastructure across multiple sites.
                      FinServ context: A zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager could allow an attacker to gain root privileges on the systems financial institutions use to manage their branch office network connectivity.
                      First seen: 05 June 2026
                      Cl0p Ransomware Group
                      When a person convicted of a financial crime fails to pay the amount ordered by a court to be repaid from their criminal proceeds, resulting in additional imprisonment as a penalty.
                      FinServ context: The FCA uses confiscation orders as part of its criminal enforcement toolkit against individuals who launder proceeds of investment fraud through the UK financial system.
                      First seen: 12 May 2026
                      Claims Management Company (CMC)
                      A regulated business that manages legal or financial claims on behalf of consumers, typically taking a fee or percentage of any compensation received.
                      FinServ context: The FCA has identified CMCs running misleading social media adverts that pose as independent financial advice to recruit claimants for motor finance cases, breaching FCA financial promotion rules.
                      First seen: 08 June 2026
                      Claude Code
                      An AI-powered coding assistant developed by Anthropic that can read, write, and execute code on a user's behalf directly from the command line.
                      FinServ context: Financial services development teams adopting Claude Code should understand that malicious repositories can trigger unintended code execution, posing a risk to sensitive trading or payment system codebases.
                      First seen: 11 May 2026
                      Claw Chain Vulnerabilities
                      A series of connected security flaws in an AI agent framework that an attacker can exploit in sequence, with each step enabling the next, ultimately achieving a much more damaging outcome than any single flaw alone.
                      FinServ context: In financial services, chained vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks are particularly dangerous because automated agents may have access to payment systems, customer data, and internal APIs, meaning a full chain exploit could have immediate financial impact.
                      First seen: 26 May 2026
                      Claw Chain Vulnerability
                      A chained sequence of security weaknesses in an AI agent framework where exploiting one flaw enables an attacker to trigger further, more serious flaws in sequence.
                      FinServ context: In a financial services AI agent deployment — such as one used for customer onboarding or fraud detection — a claw chain attack could allow credential theft, privilege escalation, and persistent backdoor access in a single attack flow.
                      First seen: 29 May 2026
                      Clearinghouse (Open Source Software Context)
                      A central platform or organisation that collects, validates, and distributes security information or software components so that multiple parties can share them reliably.
                      FinServ context: Banks adopting a shared clearinghouse for open source software can collectively vet third-party libraries before they are used in trading platforms or payment systems, reducing supply chain risk.
                      First seen: 01 June 2026
                      CLI Runtime
                      A command-line interface process that executes instructions directly on an operating system, typically with access to files, environment variables, and network resources.
                      FinServ context: In agentic AI deployments at financial firms, a spawned CLI runtime operating with elevated privileges — as demonstrated in the Cursor MCP proof-of-concept — could silently exfilt
                      ClickFix
                      A social engineering technique where victims are tricked into manually running malicious commands on their own machine, often by being shown a fake error message with instructions to "fix" a problem.
                      FinServ context: The Lazarus Group used ClickFix to target high-value individuals at organisations, a tactic that could be used against executives or developers at financial institutions to gain initial access to sensitive systems.
                      First seen: 25 April 2026
                      Client Money Safeguarding
                      The regulatory requirement to keep customer funds separate from a firm's own money so that clients are protected if the firm fails.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms must follow FCA client money rules, including segregation of duties — the Sapia case demonstrated that allowing the same staff to both make and check client money payments creates dangerous control weaknesses.
                      First seen: 24 April 2026
                      Client Money Segregation
                      The communication of an invitation or inducement to engage in a financial activity that has not been approved by an FCA-authorised person, which is a criminal offence in the UK.
                      FinServ context: Finfluencers promoting investment products on social media without FCA authorisation or approval from a regulated firm are committing unauthorised financial promotions, which can result in criminal prosecution.
                      First seen: 30 April 2026
                      Climate Transition Risk
                      The financial risk that an investment or asset loses value because the wider economy shifts away from carbon-intensive activities through new laws, technologies, or market changes.
                      FinServ context: Under the FCA's proposed simplified climate reporting rules, investment firms would need to disclose how climate transition risk — alongside physical risks like flooding — could affect the performance of retail investment products.
                      First seen: 12 June 2026
                      Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA)
                      A proposed EU law designed to regulate cloud infrastructure and AI development in Europe, reducing member states' dependence on non-European technology suppliers.
                      FinServ context: UK financial firms with EU operations will need to monitor CADA compliance requirements, particularly around where AI models and customer data are hosted.
                      First seen: 08 June 2026
                      Cloud API
                      Programming interfaces that allow software to interact with and control cloud services and resources programmatically.
                      FinServ context: Financial firms rely heavily on cloud APIs for automated trading and customer services, making API security critical to prevent unauthorized transaction execution.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Cloud Metadata Endpoint
                      A special internal web address available on cloud servers that returns sensitive configuration details about the server, including credentials and access tokens, intended only for the server itself to use.
                      FinServ context: If an attacker in a financial services cloud environment can reach a cloud metadata endpoint via a server-side request forgery vulnerability, they can steal temporary cloud credentials and pivot to access customer data stores or payment systems.
                      First seen: 23 June 2026
                      Cloud Metadata Service (IMDS)
                      A special internal endpoint available inside cloud virtual machines that provides configuration data and temporary credentials to the running instance, typically accessible only from within that machine.
                      FinServ context: If an attacker exploits an SSRF vulnerability in a bank's AI platform, they can query the cloud metadata service to steal IAM credentials and gain broader access to the firm's cloud environment.
                      First seen: 01 July 2026
                      Cobalt Strike
                      A commercial adversary simulation tool that attackers frequently repurpose to maintain persistent access and move laterally through compromised networks.
                      FinServ context: Cobalt Strike beacons deployed via loaders like Pikabot represent a significant post-compromise threat within financial services infrastructure, enabling attackers to pivot toward payment systems or customer data.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Code Injection
                      An attack where malicious code is inserted into a vulnerable application and executed by that application.
                      FinServ context: Code injection in AI-powered financial applications could allow attackers to manipulate algorithmic trading decisions or bypass fraud detection systems.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      Command Injection
                      An attack where malicious commands are inserted into application inputs that are then executed by the underlying operating system.
                      FinServ context: Banking web applications that don't properly validate user inputs could allow attackers to execute system commands that bypass financial controls or access sensitive customer databases.
                      First seen: 30 March 2026
                      Command Substitution
                      A technique where attackers embed executable commands within data that gets processed by shell interpreters.
                      FinServ context: Log analysis tools used by financial firms could execute malicious commands if transaction logs contain specially crafted command substitution payloads.
                      First seen: 09 April 2026
                      Communications Surveillance (Agentic)
                      The automated monitoring and review of digital communications — such as emails, chats, and voice — using AI agents to detect misconduct, market abuse, or policy breaches.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms are required to record and surveil communications under FCA rules, and agentic surveillance tools can process far larger volumes of messages than human reviewers, flagging suspicious trading conversations in real time.
                      First seen: 29 June 2026
                      Communications Surveillance (FinServ AI Context)
                      The automated monitoring and review of electronic communications — such as emails, chats, and voice calls — to detect regulatory breaches, misconduct, or financial crime.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms are required by the FCA to surveil employee communications, and agentic AI tools are now being deployed to investigate flagged conversations at scale, reducing manual review burden.
                      First seen: 30 June 2026
                      Compensatory Interest Rate
                      A percentage rate applied to a compensation payment to account for the financial loss a customer suffered over time by being denied a fair outcome.
                      FinServ context: The FCA's motor finance redress scheme mandates a minimum 3% per annum compensatory interest rate, setting a regulatory precedent for how redress calculations must be modelled and audited by firms' systems.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      Computationally Expensive Tool Calls
                      Actions triggered by an AI agent that consume large amounts of processing power, API quota, or money, which an attacker can deliberately cause to exhaust a firm's resources.
                      FinServ context: An attacker could inject a prompt into a financial institution's AI assistant instructing it to repeatedly query market data APIs, running up costs and degrading service for legitimate users.
                      First seen: 28 May 2026
                      COMSPEC Environment Variable
                      A Windows environment variable that tells the operating system which command interpreter (such as cmd.exe) to use when running commands.
                      FinServ context: If an attacker manipulates the COMSPEC variable in a financial firm's AI development environment, they could trick the system into executing malicious code instead of the legitimate command interpreter.
                      First seen: 19 May 2026
                      Confiscation Order
                      A court order requiring a convicted criminal to repay a specific sum of money representing the proceeds of their crimes.
                      FinServ context: In financial services fraud cases, the FCA may pursue a Confiscation Order to recover laundered funds from convicted money launderers who profited from investment fraud.
                      First seen: 11 May 2026
                      Confiscation Order Default
                      A legal situation where a convicted person fails to pay the amount required under a Confiscation Order, resulting in additional custodial punishment.
                      FinServ context: In FCA-prosecuted financial crime cases, failure to satisfy a Confiscation Order can result in significant additional prison time, serving as an enforcement mechanism to recover proceeds of investment fraud.
                      First seen: 11 May 2026
                      Connection Exhaustion
                      A denial of service technique where an attacker opens so many simultaneous connections to a server that no resources remain available for legitimate users.
                      FinServ context: Network orchestration tools used by financial firms to manage infrastructure could be rendered unavailable through connection exhaustion attacks, disrupting critical payment or trading operations.
                      First seen: 07 May 2026
                      Consolidated Tape
                      A sum of money paid by a firm to compensate affected customers as part of an FCA enforcement outcome, without the payment being formally imposed as a fine.
                      FinServ context: CACEIS UK's £31.7m voluntary payment to WealthTek clients illustrates how sub-custodian failures in client asset oversight can result in significant financial liability even where no formal penalty is levied.
                      First seen: 03 July 2026
                      Consumer Credit Journey
                      The full sequence of borrowing events a customer experiences over time, from initial credit application through repayment, default, or product switching, as recorded across financial datasets.
                      FinServ context: The FCA is using credit file data from Credit Reference Agencies to map consumer credit journeys, allowing regulators to identify systemic risk or harm patterns that individual firm-level data would miss.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      Consumer Duty
                      An FCA regulatory principle requiring financial firms to deliver good outcomes for retail customers across all their products and services.
                      FinServ context: The FCA is aligning simplified climate reporting requirements with Consumer Duty obligations, meaning firms must ensure any climate-related disclosures are genuinely useful and clear to retail investors.
                      First seen: 05 June 2026
                      Context of the Current User
                      The security permissions and access rights that belong to whoever is currently logged into the system when an attack occurs.
                      FinServ context: If malware executes in a bank employee's context, it inherits their access to customer accounts, trading systems, or sensitive financial data.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Context Poisoning
                      The practice of contaminating an AI system's working memory with malicious information to influence its future responses and behavior.
                      FinServ context: In financial AI systems, context poisoning could cause investment advisory bots to provide manipulated recommendations or customer service agents to leak confidential account information.
                      First seen: 26 March 2026
                      Continuous Penetration Testing
                      A security testing approach where systems are assessed for vulnerabilities on an ongoing basis rather than during a single scheduled engagement.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions relying on annual or bi-annual pen tests may leave hundreds of days of unvalidated exposure — continuous testing helps ensure new attack surfaces introduced by cloud migrations or system changes are caught promptly.
                      First seen: 04 June 2026
                      Continuous Verification
                      A security approach that repeatedly checks a user's identity and device trustworthiness throughout an active session, rather than only at the point of login.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms subject to Consumer Duty obligations are adopting continuous verification to detect when a legitimate session may have been compromised mid-transaction.
                      First seen: 18 June 2026
                      Contributor-Level Access
                      A low-privilege user role in WordPress that allows a user to create and edit their own content but not publish it or access administrative functions.
                      FinServ context: Attackers exploiting missing capability checks may use Contributor-level accounts — which are easy to register on many sites — to perform destructive administrative actions on financial services web properties.
                      First seen: 15 May 2026
                      Control and Provisioning of Wireless Access Points (CAPWAP)
                      A protocol that allows wireless network controllers to manage and configure multiple wireless access points from a central location.
                      FinServ context: Bank branches using centralised wireless management through CAPWAP face denial of service risks that could disrupt mobile banking services and customer Wi-Fi access.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      Cordyceps Attack (Malicious Pull Request via Fork)
                      An attack pattern where a contributor submits a pull request from a forked repository that triggers CI/CD pipelines with access to secrets, even without being merged.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using open-source components or shared developer tooling are exposed to this attack if their CI/CD pipelines grant secrets access to unreviewed pull requests from external contributors.
                      First seen: 26 June 2026
                      Corporate Bond Transparency
                      The regulatory requirement for details of bond trades — such as price and volume — to be reported and made publicly available, typically in real time.
                      FinServ context: The UK's bond consolidated tape now aggregates real-time corporate bond trade data, which has security implications around data integrity, authorised access to market data feeds, and preventing manipulation of reported prices.
                      First seen: 02 July 2026
                      Cost harvesting
                      An attack where adversaries deliberately drive up a victim's AI service costs by forcing expensive computational operations.
                      FinServ context: Attackers could target a bank's AI-powered fraud detection system with resource-intensive queries to inflate cloud computing bills and potentially degrade service performance.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      cost harvesting
                      An attack strategy focused on deliberately increasing a victim's AI service costs through resource-intensive queries rather than stealing data.
                      FinServ context: Competitors or malicious actors could target a fintech's AI-powered fraud detection system with complex queries designed to exhaust their cloud computing budget.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      Covert Network (of Compromised Devices)
                      A hidden network of devices that have been secretly taken over by attackers and used to route malicious traffic while disguising its true origin.
                      FinServ context: China-nexus threat actors have used covert networks of compromised edge devices — such as VPN appliances — to conduct espionage against financial services firms while making attribution difficult for defenders.
                      First seen: 24 April 2026
                      cPanel / WHM
                      Widely used web hosting control panel software that allows administrators to manage websites, email accounts, and server configurations through a graphical interface.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms or their managed service providers running customer portals on cPanel-based hosting must treat authentication bypass vulnerabilities as critical, as exploitation could expose client data or allow unauthorised account access.
                      First seen: 01 May 2026
                      Crafted File
                      A deliberately modified file designed to exploit vulnerabilities in software that processes it.
                      FinServ context: Attackers might send crafted financial documents to banks that trigger vulnerabilities when processed by document management or compliance systems.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Crafted Packets
                      An attack where a malicious actor intercepts and potentially modifies communications between two devices by positioning themselves between them.
                      FinServ context: In financial services, this attack could be used to intercept and manipulate communications between trading terminals and exchanges or between ATMs and banking servers.
                      First seen: 26 March 2026
                      Credential Harvesting (AI Agent Context)
                      The act of extracting stored usernames, passwords, API keys, or tokens from an AI agent's configuration files, often triggered by a prompt injection attack that instructs the agent to read and send those files.
                      FinServ context: In financial services firms deploying AI coding assistants or agentic tools, attackers can craft malicious web content that, when scraped by the agent, instructs it to exfiltrate credential files containing access keys to core banking APIs.
                      First seen: 21 May 2026
                      Credential Stuffing
                      An automated attack where stolen username and password combinations from one data breach are tried against many other websites or services, relying on people reusing the same passwords.
                      FinServ context: UK retail banks and payment platforms are frequent targets of credential stuffing because customers often reuse passwords across banking and non-financial accounts.
                      First seen: 09 June 2026
                      Credit Reference Agency (CRA)
                      An organisation that collects and holds financial history data on individuals and businesses, which lenders use to assess creditworthiness.
                      FinServ context: The FCA uses data from CRAs to build analytical models that track consumer credit journeys and identify early signs of financial distress across the market.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      Credit Underwriting (AI Context)
                      The process of assessing whether a borrower is likely to repay a loan, increasingly performed or assisted by AI models that analyse large datasets to make lending decisions.
                      FinServ context: AI-driven credit underwriting platforms used by financial firms must be governed carefully to ensure decisions are explainable, fair, and compliant with FCA consumer duty obligations.
                      First seen: 01 June 2026
                      Critical Infrastructure (Cyber Context)
                      The essential systems and services — such as energy, finance, transport and communications — whose disruption would have serious national consequences, making them high-priority targets for hostile states.
                      FinServ context: The NCSC has linked three-quarters of cyberattacks on UK critical systems to hostile states, meaning UK financial market infrastructure such as payment clearing systems is a direct target for nation-state threat actors.
                      First seen: 18 June 2026
                      Critical National Infrastructure (CNI) Penetration Testing
                      Authorised security testing performed on systems that underpin essential national services, such as energy, finance, and payments, to identify weaknesses before malicious actors can exploit them.
                      FinServ context: UK financial firms classified as CNI operators face heightened regulatory expectations around offensive security testing, meaning junior engineers should understand how pen testing findings feed into resilience obligations.
                      First seen: 01 July 2026
                      Cron State
                      The saved configuration and scheduling information that controls when automated recurring tasks run on a system.
                      FinServ context: In a financial services environment, unauthorised modification of cron state could allow an attacker to schedule persistent malicious tasks such as recurring data exfiltration from payment or customer systems.
                      First seen: 12 June 2026
                      Cross-Cluster Privilege Escalation
                      An attack where an attacker gains elevated permissions across multiple interconnected computer clusters or cloud environments.
                      FinServ context: In financial services using multi-cloud deployments, this could allow an attacker to move from a development cluster to production systems containing sensitive customer financial data.
                      First seen: 08 April 2026
                      Cross-Origin Redirect
                      When a web request is automatically sent from one website or server to a completely different domain, which can be abused to trick systems into sending sensitive data to an attacker-controlled destination.
                      FinServ context: In financial services AI deployments, a compromised MCP endpoint could use cross-origin redirects to silently forward authentication headers containing customer data or internal credentials to an attacker.
                      First seen: 17 June 2026
                      Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
                      A security mechanism that uses single-use random numbers to verify that web requests are legitimate and prevent replay attacks.
                      FinServ context: Payment processing systems rely on nonce validation to ensure each transaction request is genuine and hasn't been intercepted and replayed by fraudsters.
                      First seen: 08 April 2026
                      Cryptoasset Safeguarding
                      The regulatory requirement for firms holding customers' cryptoassets to keep those assets separate from the firm's own assets so customers can recover them if the firm fails.
                      FinServ context: Under the FCA's forthcoming UK crypto regime, a fintech offering custody of Bitcoin or stablecoins must implement cryptoasset safeguarding controls, including segregated wallets and reconciliation processes, analogous to client money rules in traditional finance.
                      First seen: 20 April 2026
                      Cryptocurrency ATM
                      A physical kiosk that allows people to buy or sell cryptocurrency using cash or a card, without going through an online exchange.
                      FinServ context: UK and US regulators are increasingly scrutinising crypto ATMs because criminals use them to receive fraud proceeds quickly and with limited traceability, bypassing standard anti-money laundering controls.
                      First seen: 28 April 2026
                      Cryptographic Signature Verification
                      A security check that confirms a message or request genuinely came from a trusted source by validating a mathematical signature attached to it.
                      FinServ context: Payment gateways like Redsys must verify cryptographic signatures on every callback to prevent attackers from injecting false payment status updates into banking or e-commerce flows.
                      First seen: 16 April 2026
                      cryptographic validation
                      The process of using mathematical algorithms to verify that data hasn't been tampered with and comes from a trusted source.
                      FinServ context: Payment systems must implement cryptographic validation on all transaction tokens to prevent fraudulent modifications of transfer amounts.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      Cryptomining (Unauthorised)
                      The use of someone else's computing resources, without their consent, to generate cryptocurrency for the attacker's benefit.
                      FinServ context: Attackers exploiting vulnerabilities in developer tools used by a fintech firm could deploy cryptominers on cloud infrastructure, driving up compute costs and degrading system performance.
                      First seen: 30 April 2026
                      CTP OS
                      Juniper Networks' operating system designed for telecommunications and service provider equipment.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using CTP OS for network infrastructure could face security risks if weak password requirements aren't properly enforced on devices handling sensitive trading data.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      Cursor (AI Code Editor)
                      An AI-powered software development tool that uses large language models to help engineers write, review, and edit code through natural language instructions.
                      FinServ context: Financial services engineering teams adopting AI coding assistants like Cursor must understand that malicious MCP servers can inject instructions that cause the tool to exfiltrate sensitive credentials or configuration files.
                      First seen: 27 April 2026
                      Cursor CLI
                      A command-line interface for the Cursor AI code editor that allows developers to interact with AI coding capabilities directly from a terminal.
                      FinServ context: Security teams in financial institutions using Cursor CLI for development should be aware it can be manipulated via prompt injection to execute shell commands and exfiltrate configuration files containing credentials.
                      First seen: 11 May 2026
                      CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures)
                      A standardised public list that assigns unique identifiers to known software security flaws, allowing organisations to track and prioritise which weaknesses need fixing.
                      FinServ context: Security teams at financial services firms use CVE identifiers to map vendor patch releases against their own asset inventory and determine remediation urgency under internal risk frameworks.
                      First seen: 20 April 2026
                      CVE-2026-20262 (Cisco SD-WAN vManage)
                      A specific, publicly catalogued security flaw in Cisco's SD-WAN management software that allows an attacker to gain full administrative control of the underlying system.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions running Cisco SD-WAN to connect branch offices and data centres must apply Cisco's emergency patch immediately, as exploitation could give attackers root-level access to network infrastructure carrying sensitive transaction data.
                      First seen: 16 June 2026
                      CVSS (Common Vulnerability Scoring System)
                      A standardised scoring system that rates the severity of a software vulnerability on a scale from 0 to 10, based on factors like how easy it is to exploit and what damage it could cause.
                      FinServ context: All four Oracle Financial Services CVEs scored 7.5 (High), meaning UK financial firms running affected versions should treat patching as urgent given the unauthenticated, network-exploitable nature of the flaws.
                      First seen: 22 April 2026
                      Cyber Capabilities (of AI Models)
                      The ability of an AI model to autonomously understand, assist with, or execute technical tasks related to attacking or compromising computer systems.
                      FinServ context: UK regulators have warned that frontier AI models now exceed what a skilled human practitioner can achieve in offensive cyber tasks, meaning a financial firm whose defences are not AI-resilient faces a materially higher threat level.
                      First seen: 20 May 2026
                      Cyber Insurance Exclusions
                      Specific conditions or types of incident written into a cyber insurance policy that the insurer will not pay out for.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms should scrutinise policy wording carefully, as some insurers are now excluding social engineering attacks such as ClickFix from coverage, leaving firms potentially exposed to uninsured losses.
                      First seen: 04 June 2026
                      Cybercrime-as-a-Service
                      A business model where criminal groups offer hacking tools, infrastructure, or expertise to other criminals for a fee, lowering the technical barrier to carrying out attacks.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms face threats not just from sophisticated nation-state actors but from relatively unskilled criminals who can rent professional-grade attack tools and infrastructure through cybercrime-as-a-service platforms.
                      First seen: 25 June 2026
                      D
                      dangerouslySetInnerHTML
                      A React property that directly inserts raw HTML into a webpage without sanitisation, creating a cross-site scripting risk if the content comes from untrusted user input.
                      FinServ context: A WordPress form plugin used by a financial services firm's contact page used `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to render stored submissions, meaning an attacker could inject scripts that steal session tokens from admin users.
                      First seen: 28 May 2026
                      Dark Web
                      A part of the internet that is not indexed by standard search engines and requires special software to access, commonly used to buy and sell stolen data and criminal services.
                      FinServ context: Stolen customer biometric data and identity documents from financial services breaches frequently appear on dark web marketplaces, enabling synthetic identity fraud.
                      First seen: 15 June 2026
                      Data Breach Notification
                      The legal or regulatory obligation to inform affected individuals and relevant authorities when personal data has been unlawfully accessed or stolen.
                      FinServ context: UK financial firms must notify the FCA and ICO within strict timeframes following a breach — delays can result in significant fines on top of any reputational damage from the incident itself.
                      First seen: 28 April 2026
                      Data Encrypted for Impact
                      A ransomware technique where attackers encrypt files and systems to disrupt operations and demand payment for decryption.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions face severe regulatory and customer impact when transaction systems or customer databases are encrypted, often forcing rapid ransom payment decisions.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Data Execution Prevention (DEP)
                      A vulnerability where data written to a buffer exceeds its allocated memory space on the program's call stack, potentially allowing code execution.
                      FinServ context: Trading applications with buffer overflow vulnerabilities could be exploited to manipulate transaction data or gain unauthorized access to financial systems.
                      First seen: 13 April 2026
                      Data Exfiltration
                      The unauthorised copying or transfer of
                      Data Leak (AI Context)
                      The unintended exposure of sensitive information caused by an AI agent or model being manip
                      Data Silo
                      A situation where data is held and used by one part of an organisation but is not accessible to or integrated with other parts, limiting the overall picture available for decision-making.
                      FinServ context: In financial institutions, data silos between fraud, identity, and transaction teams prevent a complete customer risk view, weakening both fraud detection and regulatory compliance.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      DBIR (Data Breach Investigations Report)
                      Verizon's annual industry report that analyses thousands of real-world security incidents to identify the most common attack methods, threat actors, and vulnerable industries.
                      FinServ context: The DBIR is widely referenced by security teams at UK financial services firms as a benchmark for understanding which threats — such as credential theft or exploit-based initial access — are most likely to affect their sector.
                      First seen: 21 May 2026
                      DCMTK
                      An open-source software toolkit for handling DICOM (medical imaging) data and communications.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms processing healthcare payments or insurance claims may encounter DCMTK vulnerabilities in medical data systems they interface with.
                      First seen: 07 April 2026
                      DDoS Botnet
                      A network of compromised internet-connected devices secretly controlled by an attacker and used collectively to flood a target with traffic, knocking it offline.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms, including payment processors and trading platforms, are high-value DDoS targets, and commercially available botnets-for-hire significantly reduce the cost and skill required to launch such attacks.
                      First seen: 25 May 2026
                      DDoS-for-Hire (Booter/Stresser)
                      A commercial service that sells distributed denial-of-service attack capability to paying customers, allowing even non-technical criminals to overwhelm and take down internet-facing systems.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms are high-value targets for DDoS-for-hire attacks, which can disrupt payment platforms, online banking, and trading infrastructure during peak business hours.
                      First seen: 20 April 2026
                      Deep Learning Frameworks
                      Software libraries and tools that provide the building blocks for creating and training artificial intelligence models.
                      FinServ context: Investment firms use deep learning frameworks like PyTorch to build algorithmic trading models that can analyse market patterns and execute trades automatically.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      Deepfakes
                      AI-generated synthetic media that convincingly replaces a person's appearance or voice with someone else's.
                      FinServ context: Financial criminals could use deepfakes to impersonate bank executives in video calls to authorize fraudulent wire transfers or bypass voice authentication systems.
                      First seen: 06 April 2026
                      Defense Evasion (AI Context)
                      Techniques used by attackers to avoid detection or circumvent security controls, including those built into AI systems such as content filters or behavioural guardrails.
                      FinServ context: An adversary who achieves defense evasion against an LLM deployed in a bank's advisory chatbot could extract proprietary financial data or generate fraudulent customer communications without triggering alerts.
                      First seen: 01 June 2026
                      DeFi (Decentralised Finance)
                      A system of financial services built on blockchain technology that operates without traditional intermediaries like banks, using automated smart contracts instead.
                      FinServ context: UK financial regulators are increasingly monitoring DeFi platforms because their lack of central oversight creates significant fraud and theft risks, as demonstrated by the $290 million KelpDAO heist.
                      First seen: 21 April 2026
                      Defined Benefit (DB) Pension Transfer
                      The process of moving a client's pension from a guaranteed employer-sponsored scheme into a personal pension, a regulated activity requiring specialist FCA authorisation.
                      FinServ context: A junior engineer supporting a wealth management platform must understand that DB pension transfer advice is a highly regulated activity, and any system handling such transactions must enforce strict controls around adviser authorisation and professional indemnity insurance status.
                      First seen: 13 May 2026
                      Denial of Service (DoS)
                      An attack that makes a system or service unavailable to legitimate users by exhausting its resources, such as connections, memory, or processing power.
                      FinServ context: A DoS attack against a bank's trading platform or payments infrastructure during peak hours could cause significant financial and reputational harm, triggering FCA operational resilience reporting obligations.
                      First seen: 06 May 2026
                      Dependency Chain Attack
                      An attack that targets third-party software libraries or packages that a legitimate application relies on, rather than the application itself.
                      FinServ context: AI-enabled systems in financial services that use frameworks like PyTorch or LangChain inherit risk from their entire dependency chain, meaning a compromised open-source package could silently affect a trading model or fraud detection system.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Deserialization
                      The process of converting stored data back into its original object format that a program can use.
                      FinServ context: Payment processing systems often deserialize transaction data, making them vulnerable if malicious data is crafted to execute harmful code during this conversion.
                      First seen: 30 March 2026
                      Destination Buffer
                      A specific sequence of network packets or traffic patterns that triggers malware to activate or execute commands.
                      FinServ context: Attackers could plant backdoors in financial networks that remain dormant until activated by a secret knock, allowing them to bypass monitoring systems during off-hours trading.
                      First seen: 07 April 2026
                      detailPlus Endpoint
                      A specific API endpoint in PowerJob software that processes database queries but contains a security vulnerability allowing SQL injection attacks.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using PowerJob for job scheduling could face data breaches if attackers exploit this endpoint to extract sensitive customer or transaction data.
                      First seen: 08 April 2026
                      Determine_current_user filter
                      A WordPress mechanism that determines which user account is currently logged in and active during a web request.
                      FinServ context: Compromising this filter in financial WordPress sites could allow attackers to impersonate bank administrators or access customer account management systems.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      determine_current_user filter
                      A WordPress function that decides which user account is currently active during a web request.
                      FinServ context: Attackers could exploit vulnerable plugins that override this filter to impersonate bank employees or customers without proper authentication.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      Device Trust
                      A security control that verifies whether the specific device attempting to log in meets defined security standards before granting access, rather than relying solely on credentials.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms increasingly use device trust as an additional layer of verification to reduce account takeover risk, ensuring that even valid credentials cannot be used from an unrecognised or unmanaged device.
                      First seen: 18 June 2026
                      Device-Less Allow Path
                      A SQL injection attack technique where attackers infer database information by measuring how long the database takes to respond to specially crafted queries.
                      FinServ context: Attackers could use timing attacks against banking databases to slowly extract customer account information or transaction data without triggering obvious error messages.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      DHCP Snooping
                      A security feature that monitors DHCP traffic on a network switch to prevent rogue DHCP servers and IP address conflicts.
                      FinServ context: Payment processing networks use DHCP snooping to ensure only authorised devices receive IP addresses, preventing attackers from intercepting financial transactions through network spoofing.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      Dify
                      Financial markets where large institutions such as banks, asset managers, and insurers trade financial instruments directly with each other rather than with retail consumers.
                      FinServ context: The FCA and Bank of England are exploring how tokenisation and distributed ledger technology could make wholesale markets faster and more efficient for UK financial firms.
                      First seen: 19 May 2026
                      Digital Ledger
                      A shared, tamper-resistant record of transactions or asset ownership that is maintained and synchronised across multiple participants rather than by a single central authority.
                      FinServ context: The FCA and Bank of England are exploring how digital ledgers can underpin tokenised securities in UK wholesale markets, potentially replacing legacy settlement infrastructure.
                      First seen: 27 May 2026
                      Digital Testing Environment
                      A controlled, sandboxed technical platform where firms can build and test products or solutions without affecting live systems or real customer data.
                      FinServ context: The FCA's Smart Data Accelerator uses a digital testing environment to allow firms to prototype open finance solutions safely before they influence real policy or go into production.
                      First seen: 04 May 2026
                      Digital Wallet
                      A software application that stores payment credentials and allows users to make electronic transactions without using a physical card or cash.
                      FinServ context: The FCA is investigating whether Mastercard, Visa, and PayPal engaged in anti-competitive conduct related to the funding and usage of PayPal's digital wallet in UK payment markets.
                      First seen: 11 May 2026
                      Direct Cloud VM Connections
                      Cloud-native methods for logging directly into virtual machines hosted in the cloud without traditional network access.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions using cloud infrastructure need to secure these direct connection methods to prevent unauthorized access to systems processing sensitive financial data.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Directory Traversal
                      An attack where a malicious user manipulates file path inputs to access files and directories stored outside the intended folder on a web server.
                      FinServ context: The WooCommerce WordPress plugin vulnerability (CVE-2026-14352) showed how directory traversal can expose sensitive server files at a financial services firm running e-commerce or customer-facing web applications.
                      First seen: 03 July 2026
                      Dirty Frag (Linux Privilege Escalation)
                      A class of Linux kernel vulnerability that allows a local attacker to gain elevated system privileges by exploiting flaws in how the kernel manages memory fragments or pages.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms running enterprise Linux distributions for trading systems or data platforms must patch kernel-level vulnerabilities promptly, as local privilege escalation can allow attackers who already have a foothold to take full control of a server.
                      First seen: 12 May 2026
                      Discover LLM System Information
                      An attack technique where an adversary queries an AI system to reveal details about its internal configuration, system prompt, or connected tools and data sources.
                      FinServ context: In a financial services context, an attacker discovering an LLM's system prompt could learn which internal databases or APIs the agent can access, enabling more targeted follow-on attacks against core banking systems.
                      First seen: 01 June 2026
                      Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT)
                      A type of database that is shared, synchronised, and recorded across multiple locations or institutions simultaneously rather than held in one central place.
                      FinServ context: The FCA and Bank of England are using DLT as the underlying infrastructure for their tokenisation vision for UK wholesale markets, requiring firms to understand its operational and security implications.
                      First seen: 18 May 2026
                      Djinn Stealer
                      A type of infostealer malware specifically designed to harvest credentials used to access cloud platforms and AI development environments.
                      FinServ context: In financial services, where AI and cloud tools are increasingly used for fraud detection and customer analytics, Djinn Stealer targeting AI credentials could give attackers access to sensitive model infrastructure or training data.
                      First seen: 30 June 2026
                      DLL Hijacking
                      An attack where malicious code libraries are placed in locations where legitimate applications will accidentally load them instead of the correct files.
                      FinServ context: Financial software could be compromised by placing malicious DLL files in application directories, potentially allowing transaction manipulation.
                      First seen: 09 April 2026
                      DNSSEC Validation
                      A security process that verifies the authenticity of DNS responses to ensure domain name lookups haven't been tampered with by attackers.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions performing DNSSEC validation protect against DNS poisoning attacks that could redirect customers to fraudulent banking websites during online transactions.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      Domain-Joined Server
                      A server that has been connected to and registered with a central Windows Active Directory domain, meaning it shares authentication and policy controls with other machines in the organisation.
                      FinServ context: Domain-joined backup servers in a bank's environment are a high-value target because compromising one can give attackers access to credentials and trust relationships across the wider network.
                      First seen: 10 June 2026
                      Drupal CMS
                      A widely used open-source content management system written in PHP that organisations use to build and manage websites and web applications.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using Drupal for customer-facing portals must prioritise patching SQL injection vulnerabilities, as CISA has flagged active exploitation that could expose sensitive customer data.
                      First seen: 26 May 2026
                      DTLS (Datagram Transport Layer Security)
                      A security protocol that provides encrypted, tamper-resistant communication over connectionless network channels, similar to TLS but designed for protocols that do not guarantee packet delivery.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions using real-time trading or voice-over-IP systems may rely on DTLS to secure time-sensitive data transfers, making handshake parsing flaws like integer underflows a critical patching priority.
                      First seen: 01 May 2026
                      Dual Role Conflict (Client Money Context)
                      A governance failure where the same individual or team is responsible for both making payments from a client money account and independently checking that those payments are authorised and correct.
                      FinServ context: The FCA censured Sapia because staff who could initiate payments from WealthTek client money accounts were also performing the oversight checks, removing the independent safeguard required under FCA client money rules.
                      First seen: 01 May 2026
                      Dynamic Callable Syntax
                      A programming feature that allows functions or methods to be called indirectly using variable names or expressions.
                      FinServ context: Attackers can abuse dynamic callable syntax in financial applications to bypass security filters and execute unauthorized code that manipulates transaction processing.
                      First seen: 08 April 2026
                      Dynamic Fragment Tags
                      Special HTML comments used by caching plugins to mark sections of web pages that should be processed dynamically rather than served from cache.
                      FinServ context: Vulnerable dynamic fragment implementations in financial web applications can expose security tokens, potentially allowing attackers to bypass authentication on banking portals.
                      First seen: 02 April 2026
                      Dyre
                      A banking trojan that was a predecessor to TrickBot, used to target financial institutions.
                      FinServ context: Understanding Dyre helps financial services security teams trace the evolution of banking-focused malware families that specifically target their sector.
                      First seen: 27 March 2026
                      E
                      Edge Device
                      A piece of networking hardware, such as a router, VPN gateway, or firewall, that sits at the boundary between an organisation's internal network and the internet.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms are advised to baseline traffic on edge devices and monitor VPN connections, as these are frequently targeted by nation-state actors seeking persistent covert access.
                      First seen: 24 April 2026
                      Egg Hunter Shellcode
                      A small piece of code that searches through memory to find and execute a larger payload that has been placed elsewhere.
                      FinServ context: Sophisticated attacks against financial trading platforms may use egg hunter techniques to locate and execute code that manipulates market data or steals algorithmic trading secrets.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      EKANS
                      A type of ransomware written in the Go programming language that encrypts files and demands payment for decryption.
                      FinServ context: EKANS poses particular risk to financial services as it can disrupt critical operational systems and has been used in attacks against multiple business sectors including energy and healthcare infrastructure that banks depend on.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      EKZ Credential Stealer
                      An undocumented malware tool designed specifically to harvest usernames, passwords, and session tokens from a compromised machine and send them to an attacker.
                      FinServ context: Deployment of an undocumented credential stealer like EKZ against a financial firm's endpoints could result in stolen privileged credentials being used to access core banking or payments systems.
                      First seen: 29 May 2026
                      Embedder Configuration
                      The settings that control which AI model or service is used to convert text into numerical representations (embeddings) for storage and retrieval in AI memory or search systems.
                      FinServ context: If an attacker can modify the embedder configuration in an AI system used by a financial firm, they can silently redirect sensitive data — such as customer queries or transaction details — to infrastructure they control.
                      First seen: 10 June 2026
                      End-of-Life (EoL) Device
                      Hardware or software that the manufacturer no longer supports with security updates, leaving any discovered vulnerabilities permanently unpatched.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms must maintain an asset inventory that flags EoL devices, as regulators and frameworks such as DORA treat continued use of unsupported technology as an unacceptable operational risk.
                      First seen: 23 April 2026
                      Environment Variable Injection
                      An attack technique where a malicious actor supplies dangerous operating system variables to a running process, causing it to load attacker-controlled code or alter its behaviour unexpectedly.
                      FinServ context: In financial services AI deployments, a malicious MCP server workspace configuration could inject variables like LD_PRELOAD to hijack a process and exfiltrate sensitive customer or credential data.
                      First seen: 12 May 2026
                      EPMM (Endpoint Manager Mobile)
                      Ivanti's mobile device management software used by organisations to control and secure smartphones and tablets that connect to corporate networks.
                      FinServ context: A zero-day in an MDM platform like EPMM is especially dangerous for financial firms whose staff access trading systems, customer data, or internal tools from managed mobile devices.
                      First seen: 08 May 2026
                      Equity Release
                      A financial product that allows homeowners, typically older borrowers, to access the cash value tied up in their property without having to sell it.
                      FinServ context: As the FCA encourages the growth of later life lending including equity release, firms must ensure AI models used in product recommendations meet suitability and governance standards set out under the Consumer Duty.
                      First seen: 16 June 2026
                      ERP Software (Enterprise Resource Planning)
                      Integrated business management software that handles core organisational processes such as finance, HR, and procurement from a single platform.
                      FinServ context: Oracle ERP systems used by financial institutions and universities hold highly sensitive financial and personal data, making vulnerabilities in them a high-value target for threat actors.
                      First seen: 15 June 2026
                      ERP Software Vulnerability (Oracle Context)
                      A security flaw in enterprise resource planning software, which manages core business processes like finance, HR, and supply chain across an organisation.
                      FinServ context: The ShinyHunters group exploited a zero-day in Oracle's ERP software to steal large volumes of data, highlighting how ERP systems used across financial services firms represent a high-value target due to the sensitive data they centralise.
                      First seen: 26 June 2026
                      Error-Based SQL Injection
                      A technique where a single query triggers multiple additional queries, potentially causing excessive resource consumption.
                      FinServ context: Malicious actors could exploit AI-powered financial advisory systems by crafting prompts that cause excessive database queries, driving up cloud costs and degrading service performance.
                      First seen: 13 April 2026
                      EU AI Act
                      European Union legislation that classifies AI systems by risk level and imposes legal requirements on their development and deployment, including prohibitions on certain high-risk uses.
                      FinServ context: UK financial firms operating in the EU or selling AI-driven products there must assess whether their credit-scoring, fraud-detection, or HR tools fall into the Act's high-risk categories, triggering compliance obligations.
                      First seen: 08 May 2026
                      eval() (JavaScript)
                      A JavaScript function that takes a string and executes it as code, which is dangerous because it can run arbitrary instructions supplied by an attacker.
                      FinServ context: If a financial services application's backend uses eval() to process user inputs, such as query parameters or JSON payloads, an attacker could inject and execute malicious code on the server.
                      First seen: 03 June 2026
                      Excessive Queries
                      A denial-of-service technique where an attacker sends an abnormally high volume of requests to an AI service to exhaust its resources or inflate its operating costs.
                      FinServ context: A UK bank offering AI-powered customer services could face unexpected cost spikes or service outages if an adversary floods the system with excessive queries designed to drain API budgets.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Exim Mail Transfer Agent (MTA)
                      A widely used open-source piece of software that handles the routing and delivery of emails between servers on the internet.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms running their own email infrastructure on Exim must patch critical vulnerabilities promptly, as an unauthenticated attacker exploiting a flaw could execute arbitrary code on systems handling sensitive client communications.
                      First seen: 14 May 2026
                      Exploit Involvement Rate
                      The percentage of confirmed data breaches in which attackers gained initial access by exploiting a known software vulnerability, as measured across a large dataset of incidents.
                      FinServ context: Verizon's 2026 DBIR reports that exploits now account for 31% of initial access in breaches, highlighting that financial firms must treat unpatched systems as a primary attack surface rather than a secondary concern.
                      First seen: 02 June 2026
                      Exploit Patching Lag
                      The gap in time between when a security vulnerability is publicly disclosed or patched and when organisations actually apply that fix to their systems, leaving them exposed to known attacks.
                      FinServ context: For financial services firms, prolonged patching lag is a significant regulatory and operational risk, as regulators expect timely remediation of known vulnerabilities as part of operational resilience obligations.
                      First seen: 21 May 2026
                      Exploit-to-Breach Initial Access
                      The use of publicly known or discovered software vulnerabilities as the primary method for an attacker to first gain entry into a target organisation's environment.
                      FinServ context: With exploits now involved in 31% of initial access breaches according to the Verizon DBIR, financial firms must prioritise vulnerability patching timelines, particularly for internet-facing systems handling payments or customer data.
                      First seen: 29 May 2026
                      Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP)
                      A framework used in wireless networks and point-to-point connections that provides a standard way for different authentication methods to work together.
                      FinServ context: EAP vulnerabilities could compromise the security of wireless payment terminals or mobile banking applications that rely on secure wireless authentication.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      Extortion Gang
                      A cybercriminal group that steals data or disrupts systems and then demands payment from victims under threat of publishing stolen data or continuing the attack.
                      FinServ context: The ShinyHunters extortion gang's claim of responsibility for the Instructure breach highlights the reputational and regulatory risks financial services firms face when third-party technology providers suffer data theft.
                      First seen: 04 May 2026
                      Extortion Group
                      A cybercriminal organisation that steals sensitive data and threatens to publish or sell it unless the victim pays a ransom.
                      FinServ context: The ShinyHunters extortion group breached ADT and stole data on 5.5 million people, a reminder that third-party suppliers used by financial firms can be targeted to reach large customer datasets indirectly.
                      First seen: 28 April 2026
                      F
                      F-string Validation
                      Security checks that ensure user-controlled format strings in Python code cannot execute arbitrary expressions or access sensitive variables.
                      FinServ context: AI-powered financial chatbots using LangChain prompt templates need proper f-string validation to prevent attackers from injecting code that could expose customer account information.
                      First seen: 11 April 2026
                      FACT (Framework for Agentic Commerce Trust)
                      An industry framework designed to establish rules and controls for verifying and securing financial transactions that are initiated automatically by AI agents rather than human beings.
                      FinServ context: As AI agents increasingly initiate payments on behalf of customers in financial services, frameworks like FACT are needed to ensure those transactions are properly authorised and auditable.
                      First seen: 25 April 2026
                      FCA AI Live Testing
                      A regulatory programme run by the FCA that allows selected financial firms to test AI applications in a live environment under regulatory supervision before broader deployment.
                      FinServ context: Major firms including Barclays and Lloyds Banking Group are participating in the second cohort, meaning their AI models are being evaluated for consumer safety and risk management in real operational conditions.
                      First seen: 28 April 2026
                      FCA Censure
                      A formal public statement of disapproval issued by the Financial Conduct Authority against a firm that has breached regulatory rules, stopping short of a financial penalty.
                      FinServ context: Sapia received an FCA censure alongside agreeing to a voluntary £19m payment, demonstrating that censure can accompany significant financial remediation for client money failures.
                      First seen: 04 May 2026
                      FCA Markets Practitioner Panel
                      A statutory advisory panel made up of senior figures from regulated financial services firms that provides independent feedback to the FCA on its policies and rules.
                      FinServ context: In financial services, understanding which industry panels have input into FCA rulemaking helps security and compliance teams anticipate regulatory changes before they become binding requirements.
                      First seen: 02 July 2026
                      FCA Practitioner Panel
                      A statutory panel of senior industry practitioners that provides formal input and challenge to the FCA as it develops regulatory policy.
                      FinServ context: Reappointments to the FCA Practitioner Panel matter for financial services security teams because panel feedback can directly shape upcoming rules on areas such as operational resilience, AI governance, and cyber risk.
                      First seen: 03 July 2026
                      Filesystem ACL (Access Control List)
                      A set of rules attached to files or directories that defines which users or system accounts are permitted to read, write, or execute them.
                      FinServ context: Overly permissive filesystem ACLs on automation directories in financial services infrastructure can allow attackers to plant malicious scripts that execute with SYSTEM-level privileges during scheduled tasks.
                      First seen: 16 April 2026
                      Financial Resilience Requirements (Crypto Context)
                      Mandatory rules requiring firms to hold sufficient capital and pass stress tests to prove they can absorb financial shocks without failing.
                      FinServ context: UK crypto firms seeking FCA authorisation must now demonstrate financial resilience through capital adequacy and stress testing, meaning security teams must also consider operational and financial continuity controls as part of their compliance obligations.
                      First seen: 02 July 2026
                      Financial Services Register
                      The FCA's public database listing every firm and individual authorised or registered to carry out regulated financial activities in the UK.
                      FinServ context: CACEIS UK checked the Financial Services Register on three occasions and found WealthTek was not authorised to hold certain client assets, but failed to act on this information.
                      First seen: 26 June 2026
                      Financial Theft
                      A cyber attack objective where adversaries steal money or monetary resources through various technical or social engineering methods.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms are primary targets for financial theft attacks including ransomware, cryptocurrency exploitation, and unauthorized fund transfers through compromised accounts.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Finfluencer
                      A social media influencer who promotes financial products or investment opportunities to their followers, sometimes without proper authorisation or regulation.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms must ensure any third-party social media promotions comply with FCA financial promotion rules, or they risk enforcement action similar to that taken against illegal finfluencers in the FCA's 2026 week of action.
                      First seen: 24 April 2026
                      Fingerprint Cloning
                      The process of capturing and reproducing a person's fingerprint, either physically or digitally, so it can be used to fool biometric identity verification systems.
                      FinServ context: Fraudsters purchasing biometric data on dark web marketplaces can use cloned fingerprints to bypass the identity verification checks that UK financial firms rely on during customer onboarding.
                      First seen: 16 June 2026
                      First VPN (Criminal Infrastructure Context)
                      A commercial VPN service that was covertly marketed on criminal forums to allow cybercriminals to hide their real location and identity while conducting attacks such as ransomware deployment and data theft.
                      FinServ context: Financial services threat intelligence teams should monitor for staff or contractor connections originating from infrastructure previously associated with criminal VPN services, as these may indicate compromised devices or insider threat activity.
                      First seen: 22 May 2026
                      FlashCopy Manager (FCM)
                      An IBM software component that manages point-in-time snapshot copies of storage data, used for backup and recovery purposes in enterprise environments.
                      FinServ context: A vulnerability in IBM's FlashCopy Manager that allows authentication bypass via a hardcoded credential could let an attacker impersonate legitimate backup clients at a financial institution, potentially accessing or corrupting critical data backups.
                      First seen: 23 June 2026
                      Flexible PIC Concentrators (FPCs)
                      Hardware components in network routers that process and forward data packets at high speeds.
                      FinServ context: Compromised FPCs in financial networks could allow attackers to intercept or manipulate high-frequency trading communications and payment processing traffic.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      Flow Author Role (Low-Privilege API Role)
                      A user role within an AI workflow platform that grants limited permissions, such as creating and running data flows, but is not intended to have access to internal system resources.
                      FinServ context: In financial services AI deployments, inadequate separation between low-privilege roles like flow author and system-level access can allow an attacker to pivot from a basic account to internal cloud infrastructure, such as AWS metadata endpoints holding IAM credentials.
                      First seen: 01 July 2026
                      Follow_Redirects Parameter
                      A setting in an HTTP client that instructs it to automatically follow any redirect responses from a server, fetching the final destination URL rather than stopping at the first response.
                      FinServ context: When SSRF protections only validate the initial URL and not redirect destinations, an attacker in a financial services AI platform could use this parameter to reach internal systems like cloud metadata services that expose credentials.
                      First seen: 01 July 2026
                      Foreign Currency Income Lending
                      The practice of a mortgage lender assessing and accepting repayment capacity from a borrower whose salary or income is paid in a currency other than sterling.
                      FinServ context: The FCA's proposed mortgage rule changes aim to reduce barriers for lenders to extend credit to borrowers paid in foreign currencies, requiring updated affordability and credit risk models.
                      First seen: 17 June 2026
                      FortiClient EMS (Enterprise Management Server)
                      A centralised management platform used to deploy, monitor, and manage endpoint security software across an organisation's devices from a single console.
                      FinServ context: A vulnerability in FortiClient EMS could allow attackers to bypass authentication and gain access to endpoint controls across a financial firm's entire network, potentially exposing trading systems and customer data.
                      First seen: 29 May 2026
                      FortiSandbox
                      A network security appliance made by Fortinet that isolates and analyses suspicious files or traffic in a controlled environment to detect threats before they reach production systems.
