                      FinServ context: Financial services firms should be aware of groups like ShinyHunters as they increasingly target customer databases, which in a FinServ context could contain account details, identity documents, and transaction records.
                      First seen: 25 April 2026
                      Shortcode Injection (WordPress context)
                      An attack where a malicious actor inserts WordPress shortcode tags into user-supplied content such as comments, causing the server to execute unintended functionality when the page is rendered.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms running WordPress-based public sites or portals should ensure comment and input fields do not process shortcodes, as this can allow unauthenticated visitors to store malicious scripts.
                      First seen: 24 June 2026
                      SIM Swap Attack
                      Hidden software or code secretly installed on a system that allows an attacker to bypass normal authentication and regain access to the compromised system at will.
                      FinServ context: The Mistic backdoor described in the articles was used against insurance and professional services organisations, giving attackers persistent covert access long after the initial intrusion was detected and cleaned up.
                      First seen: 25 June 2026
                      SimpleHelp
                      A remote support and remote access software product used by IT teams to connect to and manage devices remotely.
                      FinServ context: An authentication bypass in SimpleHelp could allow attackers to gain unauthenticated access to systems at financial firms that use it for helpdesk and IT support operations.
                      First seen: 30 June 2026
                      Single Sign-On (SSO)
                      A system that lets a user log in once and automatically gain access to multiple connected applications without logging in again for each one.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms use SSO to give employees access to trading platforms, CRM systems, and compliance tools from a single login, making any SSO vulnerability particularly high impact.
                      First seen: 20 May 2026
                      SIPP (Self-Invested Personal Pension)
                      A regulated firm that manages legal or financial claims on behalf of consumers, typically in exchange for a fee or percentage of any compensation awarded.
                      FinServ context: The FCA has identified CMCs running misleading social media adverts that pose as independent financial advice to recruit motor finance claimants, raising conduct and financial promotion risks for firms.
                      First seen: 17 June 2026
                      Site Admin Privilege (Cisco Secure Workload Context)
                      The highest level of administrative access within a network workload security platform, giving complete control over all policies, users, and monitored systems across the entire deployment.
                      FinServ context: A vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated attacker to gain Site Admin privileges in a financial firm's workload security tool could expose every application and data flow the tool monitors, including payment processing systems.
                      First seen: 22 May 2026
                      Smart Contract
                      A self-executing program stored on a blockchain that automatically carries out predefined actions when specific conditions are met, without requiring human intervention.
                      FinServ context: Vulnerabilities in smart contracts used by DeFi platforms can be exploited to redirect funds, as regulators in the UK consider how existing financial services law applies to smart contract-based products.
                      First seen: 21 April 2026
                      Smart Data Accelerator
                      A regulatory testing environment where financial firms can build and demonstrate technical prototypes using customer data sharing, without operating in a live production environment.
                      FinServ context: The FCA uses its Smart Data Accelerator to let firms test open finance solutions for real-world problems like mortgages and SME lending, helping shape future policy before rules are finalised.
                      First seen: 29 April 2026
                      SME (Small and Medium-Sized Enterprise)
                      A business classified by size, typically by employee count or turnover, that sits below large corporate thresholds and is often underserved by standard financial products.
                      FinServ context: The FCA is exploring how open finance can give lenders a richer, more accurate picture of SME finances, which has security implications for how SME financial data is shared, consented to, and protected.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      SMS Pumping
                      A fraud technique where attackers use a victim's messaging system to generate high volumes of SMS traffic to premium rate numbers they control to earn revenue.
                      FinServ context: Banks offering SMS-based two-factor authentication may have their messaging infrastructure exploited for SMS pumping attacks that result in unexpected telecom charges.
                      First seen: 22 March 2026
                      Sniffer
                      A program that captures and analyses network traffic passing through a network interface.
                      FinServ context: Attackers may deploy sniffers on compromised financial networks to intercept sensitive data like payment card information or banking credentials in transit.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      SOC (Security Operations Centre)
                      A centralised team and facility within an organisation responsible for continuously monitoring, detecting, and responding to cybersecurity threats in real time.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms are required under FCA guidance to maintain robust detection and response capabilities, making the effectiveness of a SOC directly relevant to regulatory compliance.
                      First seen: 27 April 2026
                      Software Dependency
                      An external piece of code or library that a software application relies on to function correctly.
                      FinServ context: Banks and fintechs using open-source libraries in their applications may unknowingly inherit compromised dependencies if attackers have tampered with packages in the supply chain.
                      First seen: 04 June 2026
                      Software Supply Chain Attack
                      A cyberattack that targets the third-party libraries, tools, or packages that an organisation's software depends on, rather than attacking the organisation directly.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions that rely on open-source packages in their trading platforms or customer-facing applications are at risk if those packages are compromised before the code reaches production.
                      First seen: 05 June 2026
                      Source Code Repository
                      A version-controlled storage system where a software company keeps the underlying code for its products.
                      FinServ context: Attackers who steal source code from a security vendor, as in the Trellix breach, may reverse-engineer products deployed in financial services firms to discover undisclosed vulnerabilities before patches are available.
                      First seen: 05 May 2026
                      Span Ports
                      Network switch ports configured to receive copies of traffic from other ports for monitoring purposes.
                      FinServ context: Attackers may compromise span ports in financial networks to capture large volumes of sensitive trading data or customer information flowing between systems.
                      First seen: 23 March 2026
                      Splunk Enterprise
                      A software platform used by security teams to collect, search, and analyse large volumes of machine-generated log and event data.
                      FinServ context: A critical actively exploited flaw in Splunk Enterprise is significant for financial firms because it sits at the heart of many security operations centres, meaning a compromise could blind a firm to other ongoing attacks.
                      First seen: 19 June 2026
                      Spoofable Token
                      A security credential that can be forged or manipulated by an attacker because the system trusts the token's contents without independently verifying who issued it.
                      FinServ context: AI agent systems in financial services that rely on server-issued tokens without cryptographic verification are vulnerable to attackers presenting false identity claims to bypass access controls.
                      First seen: 07 May 2026
                      SQL Injection
                      An attack that forces a system to use up all available memory, causing applications to crash or become unresponsive.
                      FinServ context: Memory exhaustion attacks against banking systems could disrupt online banking services during peak trading hours, preventing customers from accessing their accounts.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      SSH Host Key Validation
                      The process of verifying that an SSH server's cryptographic key matches its expected identity before establishing a secure connection.
                      FinServ context: Weak validation in banking infrastructure can allow attackers to impersonate trading systems or payment gateways during secure communications.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      ssh-mcp
                      A software package that exposes secure shell (SSH) functionality through the Model Context Protocol, allowing AI agents to execute commands on remote systems.
                      FinServ context: A command injection vulnerability in ssh-mcp (CVE-2026-7039) demonstrates the risk of connecting AI agent tooling to privileged remote access mechanisms used in financial infrastructure.
                      First seen: 27 April 2026
                      SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
                      An attack where a server is tricked into making HTTP requests to unintended internal or external destinations on behalf of the attacker.
                      FinServ context: The LobeChat vulnerability (CVE-2026-59095) demonstrated how SSRF can be used to reach cloud metadata endpoints and steal credentials — a critical risk for financial services firms hosting AI tools in cloud environments.
                      First seen: 03 July 2026
                      Stablecoin Regulatory Framework
                      A set of rules requiring firms that issue or handle stablecoins to meet defined standards around capital, reserves, and transparency so that the coins reliably maintain their pegged value.
                      FinServ context: UK financial services firms handling stablecoins must now demonstrate financial resilience including stress testing, meaning compliance and security teams need to account for crypto-specific controls alongside traditional financial safeguards.
                      First seen: 01 July 2026
                      Stack Buffer Overflow
                      A vulnerability where an application writes more data to a buffer than it can hold, potentially allowing attackers to execute malicious code.
                      FinServ context: These vulnerabilities in financial software could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to trading systems or customer account databases.
                      First seen: 28 March 2026
                      Stack-based buffer overflow
                      A memory corruption vulnerability where too much data is written to a buffer located on the program's stack, potentially allowing attackers to control program execution.
                      FinServ context: Banking applications with stack-based buffer overflows could allow attackers to bypass authentication systems or gain administrative access to financial databases.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      stack-based buffer overflow
                      A vulnerability where a program writes more data to a memory buffer than it can hold, potentially allowing attackers to execute malicious code.
                      FinServ context: Network devices protecting financial institutions could be compromised through buffer overflow attacks, allowing attackers to bypass security controls and access trading systems.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      Staking
                      The process of locking up cryptocurrency holdings to support the operation of a blockchain network, in return for rewards.
                      FinServ context: The FCA is consulting on whether firms offering cryptoasset staking services to UK customers will require authorisation, meaning security teams at FinServ firms need to understand the operational risks of staking infrastructure.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      Static Credential
                      A fixed username or password that is the same across all installations of a piece of software and never changes, making it a high-risk target once discovered by an attacker.
                      FinServ context: The use of static credentials in financial infrastructure software, such as backup or storage management systems, represents a critical control failure that regulators and auditors would expect firms to identify and remediate through regular vulnerability assessments.
                      First seen: 23 June 2026
                      Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
                      A type of attack where malicious code is permanently stored on a target server and executed when other users access the affected page or content.
                      FinServ context: In financial applications, stored XSS could allow attackers to steal customer banking credentials or manipulate transaction displays when users view infected account statements or reports.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via Payload Fragmentation
                      An attack technique where a malicious script is deliberately broken into smaller pieces across multiple input fields to bypass character-length restrictions, then reassembled and executed when the page renders.
                      FinServ context: An attacker with access to an SMS gateway's admin interface at a financial firm could use fragmented payloads to plant persistent scripts that execute whenever an administrator views the dashboard, potentially capturing credentials.
                      First seen: 21 May 2026
                      storescp
                      Apple's inter-process communication mechanism that allows different applications and system services to communicate securely on macOS.
                      FinServ context: Financial services applications on macOS systems may use XPC services for secure communication between privileged and unprivileged components.
                      First seen: 07 April 2026
                      str.startswith() (Flawed Path Validation)
                      A Python string method that checks whether a string begins with a given prefix, which when used alone for directory validation is insecure because it can be tricked by sibling directory names that share the same prefix.
                      FinServ context: Developers building internal financial services tools in Python must avoid using `str.startswith()` alone to validate file paths, as attackers can bypass it to access sensitive customer data files.
                      First seen: 28 May 2026
                      Straight-Through Processing (STP)
                      The automated handling of a financial transaction from initiation to completion without any manual intervention.
                      FinServ context: Higher STP rates in payments and onboarding indicate that identity and risk decisioning systems are functioning efficiently, reducing operational cost and human error at UK financial firms.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      Streamable-HTTP MCP Server
                      A type of server that uses HTTP streaming to deliver real-time data or responses from a Model Context Protocol endpoint, keeping a connection open rather than sending a single response.
                      FinServ context: A vulnerability in streamable-HTTP MCP servers (CVE-2026-53840) could allow attackers to steal API keys or tenant-routing credentials used by financial services firms running AI agent infrastructure.
                      First seen: 17 June 2026
                      Stress Testing (Capital Adequacy Context)
                      A regulatory exercise where a firm models extreme but plausible adverse scenarios to check whether it holds enough capital and liquidity to survive them.
                      FinServ context: The FCA's new crypto framework requires all authorised crypto firms to conduct stress testing, meaning security engineers should understand how operational incidents — such as a cyber attack — could themselves trigger a stress-test failure.
                      First seen: 02 July 2026
                      Structured Exception Handling (SEH)
                      A Windows mechanism that allows programs to handle runtime errors and exceptions in a controlled way.
                      FinServ context: Attackers exploit SEH buffer overflows in financial software to execute malicious code that could steal trading data or manipulate transaction processing systems.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      Structured Exception Handling (SEH) Exploitation
                      An attack technique that exploits Windows' error handling mechanism by overwriting exception handler addresses to redirect program execution to malicious code.
                      FinServ context: SEH exploitation in financial software could bypass security controls like Data Execution Prevention (DEP) to execute unauthorized code in trading platforms or payment systems.
                      First seen: 13 April 2026
                      Sub-Custodian
                      A financial institution appointed by a primary custodian to physically hold and safeguard client assets on their behalf.
                      FinServ context: In the WealthTek case, CACEIS UK acted as sub-custodian, meaning it was responsible for keeping client assets safe but failed to act on red flags about WealthTek's authorisation status.
                      First seen: 26 June 2026
                      Sub-Custodian Oversight Failure
                      A regulatory breach where a firm acting as sub-custodian — safekeeping assets on behalf of another firm's clients — fails to act on warning signs that the appointing firm is misusing those assets.
                      FinServ context: CACEIS UK's FCA censure illustrates that sub-custodians in UK asset servicing have a positive duty to escalate concerns when the Financial Services Register shows an appointing firm lacks authorisation to hold client assets.
                      First seen: 29 June 2026
                      Subscriber-Level Access
                      The lowest privilege level assigned to a registered user of a web application, typically granting only the ability to view content rather than manage or configure the system.
                      FinServ context: In a WordPress-based customer-facing portal used by a FinTech, a vulnerability that can be exploited at subscriber level is especially dangerous because any registered customer could trigger it.
                      First seen: 20 May 2026
                      Supabase RPC
                      A way of calling custom database functions directly over a web API in the Supabase platform, allowing applications to execute server-side logic without writing raw SQL.
                      FinServ context: If an authenticated role is granted EXECUTE permission on a sensitive billing function, a malicious actor could invoke it via Supabase RPC to manipulate financial records without going through normal application controls.
                      First seen: 22 June 2026
                      superAdminAuthMiddleware
                      An API function that either creates a new user account or updates an existing one based on whether the user already exists.
                      FinServ context: Poorly secured upsertUser endpoints in banking systems could allow attackers to escalate privileges or take over customer accounts containing financial data.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Superhacker AI Model
                      Files or data stores that an AI agent uses to retain information between sessions or tasks, which can contain sensitive credentials or context gathered during previous interactions.
                      FinServ context: A vulnerability in how Anthropic's Claude handles memory files demonstrates that financial firms deploying AI agents must treat memory storage as a sensitive asset subject to the same access controls as credential vaults.
                      First seen: 06
                      Supply Chain Attack (via Malicious Pull Request)
                      An attack where a threat actor submits code contributions to open source projects with the intent of introducing vulnerabilities or malicious functionality into software that others will use.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms that rely on open source components such as Apache or Python libraries could unknowingly deploy malicious code if their development teams do not review third-party pull requests carefully.
                      First seen: 24 June 2026
                      Supply Chain Risk (in software context)
                      The risk that a weakness or compromise in a third-party software tool, library, or service provider can be used as a pathway to attack the organisations that depend on it.
                      FinServ context: The Bomgar RMM exploitation and the ATLAS AI Software technique both demonstrate how financial firms can be compromised not through their own code but through trusted third-party tools such as remote management software or AI
                      Synthetic Dataset
                      A dataset made up of artificially generated data that mimics the statistical properties of real data, without containing any actual personal or sensitive records.
                      FinServ context: The FCA and Turing Institute developed a synthetic dataset to let firms test and improve money laundering detection models without exposing real customer financial data.
                      First seen: 25 April 2026
                      SYSTEM Privileges
                      The highest level of access on a Windows computer, above even a standard administrator account, giving complete control over the entire operating system.
                      FinServ context: The MiniPlasma zero-day exploit targets fully patched Windows systems, meaning a junior analyst at a financial firm should understand that even up-to-date endpoints can be vulnerable to full system compromise.
                      First seen: 18 May 2026
                      System Prompt
                      A set of hidden instructions provided to an AI model at the start of a conversation that defines its behaviour, role, persona, and what it is or is not permitted to do.
                      FinServ context: In a financial services AI assistant, the system prompt may contain instructions restricting the model from disclosing proprietary pricing data or customer information, making it a high-value target for extraction via prompt injection attacks.
                      First seen: 27 May 2026
                      T
                      Tactics, Techniques and Procedures (TTPs)
                      A structured description of how a cyber attacker operates — their overall goals, the specific methods they use to achieve them, and the detailed steps they follow during an attack.
                      FinServ context: Understanding a ransomware group's TTPs allows a financial services firm's threat intelligence team to tune detections and controls specifically to the behaviours that group is known to use against the sector.
                      First seen: 14 May 2026
                      Taipy
                      An open-source Python framework used to build data science and AI web applications, which like any web framework can contain security vulnerabilities if not properly patched.
                      FinServ context: A path traversal flaw in Taipy's ElementLibrary could allow an attacker to read files outside the intended directory on a server hosting AI-powered financial analytics tools.
                      First seen: 28 May 2026
                      Task Scheduler Exploitation
                      A firm or individual that is permitted to carry out regulated financial activities on behalf of, and under the responsibility of, an FCA-authorised firm known as the principal.
                      FinServ context: The Sapia and WealthTek case illustrates that a principal firm bears regulatory responsibility for the conduct and client money handling of its appointed representatives, making oversight and controls critical.
                      First seen: 30 April 2026
                      TCFD (Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures)
                      An international framework that sets out how organisations should report on the financial risks and opportunities arising from climate change.
                      FinServ context: UK investment firms have been required to produce TCFD-aligned reports on their products, though the FCA is now proposing to replace these with simpler, more targeted disclosures to reduce compliance costs.
                      First seen: 05 June 2026
                      TCFD Product-Level Reporting
                      A disclosure requirement where investment firms must report climate-related financial risks at the level of individual investment products, following the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures framework.
                      FinServ context: The FCA is proposing to replace detailed TCFD product-level reports with simpler climate risk summaries aimed at retail investors, saving firms an estimated £20 million annually.
                      First seen: 08 June 2026
                      TCP Server Exposure
                      A situation where a device or application unintentionally opens a network port that accepts incoming connections, which an attacker can connect to and abuse.
                      FinServ context: A mobile banking app running on a device with a hidden TCP server exposure could allow a malicious app installed on the same device to intercept sensitive financial data or bypass security controls.
                      First seen: 20 May 2026
                      Tech Sovereignty
                      A policy goal where a country or region seeks to reduce its dependence on foreign-owned technology infrastructure, software, and hardware for critical services.
                      FinServ context: The EU's tech sovereignty package directly affects UK financial firms operating in Europe, who may face pressure to migrate cloud workloads away from US or Chinese providers.
                      First seen: 08 June 2026
                      TechSprint
                      A time-limited collaborative event where technologists, regulators, and industry participants work together intensively to build prototype solutions to a defined problem.
                      FinServ context: The FCA ran two TechSprints between November 2025 and February 2026, working with 17 firms to prototype open finance solutions as part of its Smart Data Accelerator programme.
                      First seen: 29 April 2026
                      Telegram-Based Criminal Service
                      An illicit service operated through the Telegram messaging app, used by criminals to sell attack tools, stolen data, or fraud capabilities to other bad actors with minimal traceability.
                      FinServ context: Anti-fraud and threat intelligence teams at UK financial firms increasingly monitor Telegram channels as a source of early warning about phishing kits or credential theft services targeting their customers or staff.
                      First seen: 25 May 2026
                      Template Literals
                      A JavaScript syntax feature that allows strings to be built dynamically by embedding expressions inside backtick-quoted text, which attackers can abuse to split and reassemble malicious code to evade input-length checks.
                      FinServ context: In financial services web portals and admin dashboards, template literals can be exploited in stored cross-site scripting attacks to execute malicious scripts within administrative sessions, potentially exposing customer data or system controls.
                      First seen: 21 May 2026
                      Template Tag
                      A placeholder in web applications that gets replaced with dynamic content when pages are rendered.
                      FinServ context: Malicious template tags could be exploited to expose sensitive financial data or authentication tokens in banking web applications.
                      First seen: 28 March 2026
                      Temporary Elevated Cloud Access
                      A cloud security mechanism that allows accounts to temporarily request additional permissions beyond their normal daily access rights.
                      FinServ context: Financial institutions use this to limit privilege exposure, but attackers may abuse these permission configurations to gain unauthorized access to sensitive trading systems or customer data.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Tenant Ownership Check
                      A security control that verifies a user only has permission to access or modify resources belonging to their own organisation within a multi-tenant system.
                      FinServ context: In financial services SaaS platforms, missing tenant ownership checks can allow one firm's staff to access or redirect data belonging to another client firm, breaching data protection obligations.
                      First seen: 19 May 2026
                      Tenant-Routing Credentials
                      Authentication details that tell a multi-tenant system which specific customer or organisation's environment a request belongs to and should be directed to.
                      FinServ context: Leaking tenant-routing credentials in a banking platform could allow an attacker to access or impersonate another firm's data environment within a shared cloud infrastructure.
                      First seen: 17 June 2026
                      Threat Intelligence Platform
                      A centralised system that collects, analyses, and shares information about cyber threats to help security teams identify and respond to attacks faster.
                      FinServ context: Visa's launch of a threat intelligence platform illustrates how payment networks are investing in proactive cyber defence to protect financial institutions from emerging fraud and attack patterns.
                      First seen: 03 July 2026
                      Time-based Blind SQL Injection
                      A SQL injection attack that uses database delay functions to infer information based on response times.
                      FinServ context: Attackers might use timing delays in financial applications' database responses to slowly extract sensitive customer or transaction data.
                      First seen: 27 March 2026
                      Time-Based SQL Injection
                      A specific sequence of network packets that must be received before a backdoor will activate and accept commands.
                      FinServ context: Advanced persistent threats against financial institutions might use secret knock patterns to avoid detection by network monitoring systems.
                      First seen: 09 April 2026
                      Tokenisation
                      The process of creating a digital record of a real-world asset (such as a share or bond) on a digital ledger so it can be traded or managed electronically.
                      FinServ context: UK regulators are encouraging financial firms to explore tokenisation of wholesale market assets like securities and currency to make settlement faster and reduce operational costs.
                      First seen: 18 May 2026
                      Tokenisation (Wholesale Markets)
                      The process of creating a digital representation of a real-world financial asset, such as a bond or share, recorded on a digital ledger to make it tradeable electronically.
                      FinServ context: The FCA and Bank of England are actively shaping a regulatory framework for tokenisation in UK wholesale markets, making it essential for financial services engineers to understand both the technology and its security implications.
                      First seen: 02 June 2026
                      Tokenised Securities
                      A criminal organisation that steals sensitive data and threatens to publicly release it unless the victim pays a ransom, sometimes without deploying ransomware at all.
                      FinServ context: Extortion groups such as ShinyHunters represent a growing threat to financial services firms, where the disclosure of customer data could trigger FCA breach notification obligations and significant reputational damage.
                      First seen: 27 May 2026
                      Tool Calls
                      When an AI system invokes external functions, APIs, or services to complete a task beyond text generation.
                      FinServ context: AI assistants in banking might make tool calls to check account balances or transfer funds, making them targets for manipulation to trigger unauthorized financial operations.
                      First seen: 17 April 2026
                      Tool Data Poisoning
                      A scenario where a single request triggers multiple subsequent database or API queries, potentially overwhelming system resources.
                      FinServ context: Malicious prompts could cause AI-powered banking chatbots to generate excessive database queries, disrupting customer service operations and increasing cloud costs.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      Tool_Mode Exposure (Agentic AI Context)
                      A configuration setting in an AI agent framework that makes a tool directly callable by external or user-controlled inputs, increasing the risk that a malicious prompt could trigger unintended actions.
                      FinServ context: In financial services AI deployments, enabling tool_mode on components that make HTTP requests without strict validation can allow prompt injection attacks to reach internal systems holding customer account data.
                      First seen: 01 July 2026
                      Trace Configuration
                      Settings within an AI application that define where and how the model's activity logs and conversation data are sent for observability and auditing.
                      FinServ context: Unauthorised changes to trace configuration in a financial firm's AI system could silently route transaction data or customer messages to a malicious third party.
                      First seen: 19 May 2026
                      Traffic Duplication
                      A technique where attackers configure network devices to copy and redirect network traffic to systems they control for monitoring or data theft.
                      FinServ context: An attacker could mirror network traffic from payment processing systems to capture credit card transactions, account transfers, or other sensitive financial data flowing through a bank's network.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Traffic Mirroring
                      A network feature that copies and forwards network traffic to additional destinations for monitoring or analysis purposes.
                      FinServ context: Attackers could configure traffic mirroring on compromised bank network devices to secretly copy all payment card transactions to systems they control.
                      First seen: 22 March 2026
                      Trans-national Organised Crime Group
                      A criminal network operating across multiple countries that coordinates illegal
                      TrickBot
                      A sophisticated banking trojan malware written in C++ that steals financial credentials and personal information from infected computers.
                      FinServ context: TrickBot specifically targets banking websites and financial institutions across North America, Australia, and Europe to harvest login credentials and conduct fraudulent transactions.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Trigona Ransomware
                      A type of ransomware — malicious software that encrypts victims' data and demands payment — that has been observed using custom-built tools to steal data before encrypting it.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms are at risk from ransomware strains like Trigona that combine data theft with encryption, meaning attackers can threaten to publish stolen customer or transaction data even if backups prevent operational disruption.
                      First seen: 24 April 2026
                      Trojan spyware
                      Malicious software that disguises itself as legitimate while secretly stealing information and providing unauthorized access to systems.
                      FinServ context: Trojan spyware like TrickBot specifically targets banking credentials and financial data, making it a significant threat to financial institutions and their customers.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Trust Modification
                      An attack technique where adversaries alter authentication trust relationships between different domains or cloud tenants to gain unauthorized access.
                      FinServ context: Banks using federated identity systems across multiple subsidiaries or cloud tenants could face privilege escalation if trust relationships are maliciously modified.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Trusted-Domain Prefix Match Bypass
                      A flawed security check that only verifies the beginning of a web address, allowing an attacker to craft a domain that starts with a trusted name but redirects to a malicious site.
                      FinServ context: Financial services dashboards and reporting tools that rely on allowlists to control outbound connections can be bypassed this way, enabling server-side request forgery to internal systems.
                      First seen: 11 June 2026
                      TrustFall Attack
                      A technique where a malicious code repository triggers automatic code execution in an AI coding assistant with little or no warning to the user.
                      FinServ context: AI-assisted development tools used by financial services engineers could be exploited via malicious repositories to execute unauthorised commands on developer workstations without meaningful user confirmation.
                      First seen: 11 May 2026
                      Typosquatting
                      A technique where an attacker registers a domain name or package name that is a deliberate misspelling or close variation of a legitimate one, to trick users or systems into connecting to it.
                      FinServ context: APT41 used typosquatting to disguise command-and-control communications, a tactic that could similarly be used to intercept credentials or data from financial services cloud environments.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      U
                      U
                      Ubiquiti UniFi OS
                      An operating system that runs on Ubiquiti network hardware such as routers and access points, which was found to have three chained vulnerabilities allowing unauthenticated attackers to gain full root control.
                      FinServ context: A financial firm using UniFi hardware for office or branch networking could have its entire network infrastructure compromised if these vulnerabilities remain unpatched.
                      First seen: 09 June 2026
                      Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution
                      A vulnerability that allows an attacker to run arbitrary commands on a target system without needing any valid login credentials.
                      FinServ context: Unauthenticated RCE in a core banking or HR platform is treated as a critical-severity finding under FCA operational resilience expectations because it requires no prior access to cause severe harm.
                      First seen: 12 June 2026
                      Unauthorised Financial Promotion
                      Marketing or advertising of a financial product or service that has not been approved by an FCA-authorised person, which is a criminal offence in the UK.
                      FinServ context: Social media posts by finfluencers promoting investments without FCA authorisation constitute unauthorised financial promotions, exposing both the promoter and any associated firms to criminal prosecution and regulatory sanction.
                      First seen: 24 April 2026
                      Uncontrolled Search Path Element
                      A vulnerability where an application searches for critical files or libraries in locations that an attacker can influence, allowing malicious code to be loaded instead of the legitimate file.
                      FinServ context: If a financial services workstation runs installer software vulnerable to uncontrolled search path exploitation, a low-privileged attacker could substitute a malicious library and execute code under the victim's account.
                      First seen: 16 April 2026
                      Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM)
                      A Cisco platform that manages voice, video, and messaging services across an organisation's network infrastructure.
                      FinServ context: A critical vulnerability in Cisco Unified CM with a publicly available proof-of-concept exploit poses a serious patching priority for financial institutions that use the platform for internal communications.
                      First seen: 05 June 2026
                      Unified Trust Layer
                      An industry framework that establishes shared rules for verifying identity, authorisation, and liability when automated software agents initiate financial transactions on behalf of users.
                      FinServ context: As AI agents begin to execute payments autonomously, UK payment firms need a unified trust layer to ensure accountability and fraud controls apply to agent-initiated transactions just as they do to human ones.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
                      Uninitialized Pointer Access
                      A software flaw where a program uses a memory pointer before it has been assigned a valid memory address, causing unpredictable or malicious code execution.
                      FinServ context: In financial services software, an uninitialized pointer access vulnerability in a CAD or document-parsing component could be exploited by an attacker to execute arbitrary code on a workstation used by staff handling sensitive data.
                      First seen: 13 May 2026
                      Unverified Password Change
                      A vulnerability where a system allows a user to change their password without first confirming their current password, removing a key authentication check.
                      FinServ context: In financial services platforms, an unverified password change vulnerability like CVE-2025-71328 in Flowise could allow an attacker who hijacks an authenticated session to permanently lock out the legitimate user and take over the account.
                      First seen: 26 June 2026
                      upsertUser endpoint
                      A software function that either updates an existing user account or creates a new one if it doesn't already exist.
                      FinServ context: Vulnerable upsertUser endpoints in financial applications could allow attackers to escalate privileges and gain administrative access to customer accounts or trading systems.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      Upstream-Aware Security (Open Source Context)
                      The use of autonomous AI agents to automatically monitor, analyse, and investigate employee communications for potential misconduct or policy breaches.
                      FinServ context: In UK financial services firms, regulators expect surveillance of digital communications for market abuse and conduct risk, and agentic AI tools like Shield's AmplifAI are now automating this process at scale.
                      First seen: 02 July 2026
                      User Datagram Protocol (UDP)
                      A network communication protocol that sends data packets without establishing a connection or guaranteeing delivery.
                      FinServ context: UDP-based attacks against financial network infrastructure can cause denial of service conditions that disrupt high-frequency trading systems.
                      First seen: 02 April 2026
                      user_activation_key
                      A unique code generated by web applications to verify user identity during account creation or password reset processes.
                      FinServ context: Compromised user activation keys in banking applications could allow attackers to take over customer accounts without knowing the original passwords.
                      First seen: 24 March 2026
                      user_pass Overwrite
                      An attack technique where an attacker supplies an arbitrary user identifier to a form that updates account fields, replacing the target account's password and taking control of it.
                      FinServ context: A subscriber-level employee at a financial firm could exploit a missing authorisation check to overwrite an administrator's password and gain access to sensitive regulatory reporting systems.
                      First seen: 28 May 2026
                      UTM Parameters
                      Tracking codes added to URLs that help identify where website traffic comes from, such as email campaigns or social media posts.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms use UTM parameters to track marketing effectiveness, but attackers can inject malicious scripts through these parameters to compromise analytics dashboards.
                      First seen: 17 April 2026
                      V
                      Variable Income Affordability Assessment
                      A mortgage lender's method of evaluating whether a borrower can afford repayments when their income fluctuates rather than being a fixed monthly salary.
                      FinServ context: Proposed FCA rule changes would require lenders to build systems that handle variable income data — such as freelance or gig economy earnings — creating new data validation and fraud-detection challenges for FinTech platforms.
                      First seen: 11 June 2026
                      Veeam Backup & Replication
                      A widely-used enterprise software product that creates and manages backups of servers, virtual machines, and data to allow recovery after an incident.
                      FinServ context: A compromise of backup infrastructure at a financial services firm could allow an attacker to destroy recovery points before deploying ransomware, making restoration impossible.
                      First seen: 10 June 2026
                      Vibe Coding
                      A development style where a programmer uses AI tools to generate code rapidly with minimal manual review, relying heavily on the AI's output rather than careful human oversight.
                      FinServ context: Vibe coding in a financial services engineering team creates risk because AI-generated code may introduce vulnerabilities that bypass internal security review processes if not properly governed under SM&CR accountability frameworks.
                      First seen: 18 June 2026
                      Vibe Coding Spectrum
                      A framework for calibrating how much human oversight is applied to AI-assisted code generation depending on the risk level of the code being produced.
                      FinServ context: In financial services, code that touches payment processing or customer data should sit at the high-oversight end of the vibe coding spectrum, whereas low-risk internal tooling may require less scrutiny.
                      First seen: 19 June 2026
                      Vibe Hacking
                      An emerging attack style where threat actors use AI agents to automatically generate custom hacking tools and attack scripts on demand, rather than relying on pre-written malware.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms in Latin America have already been targeted by campaigns using AI-generated custom tools, suggesting that the barrier to launching sophisticated, tailored attacks against banks is rapidly falling.
                      First seen: 14 May 2026
                      VirtualAlloc
                      A Windows API function that reserves or commits virtual memory pages, commonly abused by attackers to allocate executable memory for shellcode.
                      FinServ context: Malware targeting financial institutions may use VirtualAlloc to create executable memory regions for deploying banking trojans or credential harvesting code.
                      First seen: 13 April 2026
                      VLAN Leakage
                      A network security breach where data packets intended for one virtual network segment accidentally cross into another segment.
                      FinServ context: In banking networks, VLAN leakage could allow customer transaction data from retail banking systems to cross into corporate banking networks, violating data segregation requirements.
                      First seen: 25 March 2026
                      vLLM
                      A high-throughput and memory-efficient inference engine for serving large language models.
                      FinServ context: Attackers might use vLLM to efficiently host malicious AI models in cloud environments to target financial services applications.
                      First seen: 27 March 2026
                      vm.runInNewContext() / Node.js VM Sandbox
                      A JavaScript execution environment designed to run untrusted code in an isolated container, though it does not provide true security isolation from the host process.
                      FinServ context: A financial services firm running Node.js-based transaction processing or API services could be exposed if user-supplied data is passed into a VM sandbox that attackers can escape to execute arbitrary code on the host.
                      First seen: 03 June 2026
                      vm2 (Node.js Sandbox Library)
                      A JavaScript library designed to run untrusted code in an isolated environment, preventing it from accessing the host system — a sandbox escape vulnerability means that isolation can be broken.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using Node.js-based microservices or fintech platforms that rely on vm2 for code isolation risk full host system compromise if the sandbox escape is left unpatched.
                      First seen: 07 May 2026
                      Voluntary Payment (FCA Enforcement Context)
                      A time-limited collaborative event where firms, regulators, and technologists work together intensively to build prototype solutions to a defined policy or regulatory problem.
                      FinServ context: The FCA uses TechSprints as part of its Smart Data Accelerator to test open finance concepts such as data sharing for mortgages and SME lending in a safe, supervised digital environment before any policy changes are made.
                      First seen: 05 May 2026
                      VS Code GitHub Token Theft
                      A zero-day vulnerability in the Visual Studio Code editor that allows an attacker to steal a developer's GitHub authentication token simply by getting them to click a malicious link.
                      FinServ context: A junior developer at a UK bank who uses VS Code to work with internal code repositories could inadvertently expose source code or secrets if their GitHub token is stolen via this technique.
                      First seen: 03 June 2026
                      Vulnerability Alert Latency
                      The delay between a vulnerability being discovered and a security team being notified about it, during which attackers may already be exploiting it.
                      FinServ context: In financial services, where regulatory expectations around patching timelines are strict, high alert latency directly increases the window of exposure to known exploits.
                      First seen: 02 June 2026
                      Vulnerability Chaining
                      A technique where an attacker combines two or more individually lower-severity security weaknesses together to achieve a much more damaging outcome, such as full system takeover.
                      FinServ context: In financial services environments, vulnerability chaining means a single unpatched system running multiple flawed components could give an attacker complete control even if no single flaw is rated critical on its own.
                      First seen: 09 June 2026
                      Vulnerability Glut
                      The danger that code written or suggested by AI tools contains security flaws, because the AI may reproduce insecure coding patterns from its training data without warning the developer.
                      FinServ context: Development teams at fintech firms and banks using AI code assistants to accelerate software delivery may unknowingly introduce vulnerabilities into payment systems or APIs, which attackers can later discover and exploit.
                      First seen: 25 May 2026
                      W
                      Watchdog Timer
                      A security flaw where network traffic intended for one virtual network segment accidentally gets forwarded to another segment.
                      FinServ context: VLAN leakage in financial institutions could allow attackers to intercept sensitive trading data or payment information by accessing packets from isolated network segments.
                      First seen: 26 March 2026
                      Weaver E-cology
                      An enterprise office automation and workflow management platform widely used in organisations to handle internal approvals, documents, and collaboration.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using similar office automation platforms should treat active exploitation of critical vulnerabilities as an urgent patching priority, as these systems often hold sensitive internal approvals and communications.
                      First seen: 05 May 2026
                      Web Shell
                      A malicious script uploaded to a web server that gives an attacker remote control over that server through a browser interface.
                      FinServ context: A web shell deployed on a financial firm's learning management or customer portal server could give attackers persistent access to internal systems and customer data.
                      First seen: 27 May 2026
                      WebSocket Sessions
                      Persistent two-way communication connections between a client and server that remain open for real-time data exchange.
                      FinServ context: Trading platforms use WebSocket sessions for live market data feeds, but if these sessions aren't properly terminated when access is revoked, former employees could continue accessing sensitive financial information.
                      First seen: 01 April 2026
                      Wholesale Markets
                      Financial markets where large institutions trade high-value instruments with each other, as opposed to retail markets that serve individual consumers.
                      FinServ context: UK financial firms operating in wholesale secondary markets face FCA oversight to ensure fair competition and proper market functioning.
                      First seen: 04 May 2026
                      Wholesale Markets (Financial Services Context)
                      The part of financial markets where large institutions — such as banks, asset managers, and insurers — trade high-value instruments like bonds, derivatives, and foreign exchange with each other, rather than with individual consumers.
                      FinServ context: The FCA and Bank of England's tokenisation initiative specifically targets wholesale markets, where DLT could make settlement of securities trades faster and reduce counterparty risk.
                      First seen: 21 May 2026
                      Wholesale Markets (Tokenisation Context)
                      An AI agent framework used to build and deploy autonomous AI agents, which has been found to contain a chain of vulnerabilities allowing credential theft, privilege escalation, and persistent access.
                      FinServ context: Financial firms evaluating or deploying AI agent frameworks like OpenClaw must conduct thorough security assessments before production use, as chained vulnerabilities can give attackers persistent footholds within automated financial workflows.
                      First seen: 26 May 2026
                      Windows Cryptographic Services
                      A built-in Windows component that handles encryption, decryption, and secure storage of cryptographic material such as keys and certificates.
                      FinServ context: A vulnerability here could allow an attacker who has already gained limited access to a UK bank's Windows server to read stored cryptographic secrets and escalate to a higher-privilege account.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Windows Management Services
                      A collection of Windows operating system services that allow administrators to manage and automate system configuration and monitoring tasks.
                      FinServ context: A race condition vulnerability in Windows Management Services could allow an insider threat or compromised account at a financial firm to escalate privileges on critical infrastructure servers.
                      First seen: 15 April 2026
                      Windows Netlogon
                      A Windows service that handles authentication between computers and domain controllers in a corporate network.
                      FinServ context: A critical remote code execution flaw in Netlogon could allow attackers to compromise domain authentication infrastructure at a financial firm, potentially granting access to core banking systems.
                      First seen: 02 June 2026
                      Wire Fraud Conspiracy
                      A US federal criminal charge covering schemes that use electronic communications — such as SMS, email, or internet services — to defraud victims of money or property.
                      FinServ context: Scattered Spider member Tyler Buchanan pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy after using SMS phishing to compromise technology companies and steal cryptocurrency, a reminder that cybercrime targeting financial assets carries serious criminal liability across jurisdictions.
                      First seen: 22 April 2026
                      Wireshark-MCP
                      An open-source tool that connects a packet-capture program (Wireshark) to an AI model via the Model Context Protocol, allowing an AI agent to analyse network traffic.
                      FinServ context: If deployed in a financial services SOC to assist AI-driven threat hunting, a remote OS command injection flaw in the tool could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the analyst's workstation.
                      First seen: 05 May 2026
                      Wizard Spider
                      A cybercriminal group known for developing and operating banking trojans and ransomware campaigns.
                      FinServ context: This threat actor group has specifically targeted financial services firms across multiple continents with sophisticated banking malware.
                      First seen: 27 March 2026
                      Worker Thread Starvation
                      A denial of service condition where all available processing threads on a server are kept busy handling malicious or expensive requests, preventing legitimate requests from being processed.
                      FinServ context: An attacker sending crafted XML to a financial firm's SAML authentication endpoint could starve worker threads and prevent staff or customers from logging into critical services during peak trading hours.
                      First seen: 20 May 2026
                      Workspace Filter
                      A security check in a multi-tenant application that ensures a user's request only returns data belonging to their own workspace or organisation, preventing them from seeing other customers' data.
                      FinServ context: A missing workspace filter in a financial services AI platform, such as one used to manage chatbot configurations, could allow one firm's API key to expose the system prompts, credentials, and settings of entirely unrelated firms using the same platform.
                      First seen: 23 June 2026
                      Workspace Plugins
                      Software extensions that add functionality to development environments and are automatically loaded from specific directories.
                      FinServ context: Financial software developers using tools like OpenClaw could unknowingly execute malicious code when workspace plugins are automatically loaded from untrusted repositories containing trading algorithms or banking applications.
                      First seen: 01 April 2026
                      wp_debug_troubleshoot_simulate_user
                      A WordPress cookie parameter that allows a plugin to simulate being logged in as a different user for debugging purposes.
                      FinServ context: Financial services WordPress sites using debugging plugins could be compromised if attackers manipulate this cookie to gain administrator access to customer portals or internal systems.
                      First seen: 31 March 2026
                      wp_unslash()
                      A WordPress function that removes backslashes from data, which can inadvertently remove security protections and enable SQL injection attacks.
                      FinServ context: Financial services firms using WordPress for customer portals or marketing sites may be vulnerable to data breaches if this function undermines SQL injection protections.
                      First seen: 14 April 2026
                      WRL / TIF File (Crafted File as Attack Vector)
                      A legitimate file format — in this case a 3D world or image file — that has been specially constructed by an attacker to contain malicious content that exploits a vulnerability when the file is opened by an application.
                      FinServ context: Financial analysts and quant teams who use specialist design or data-visualisation tools may unknowingly open crafted files received via email or shared drives, triggering code execution on their workstations.
                      First seen: 27 May 2026
                      X
                      X509 Keys
                      Digital certificates that use the X.509 standard format to verify identity and enable secure communications.
                      FinServ context: In cloud environments, attackers may add malicious x509 certificates to service accounts to maintain persistent access to financial data and systems.
                      First seen: 23 March 2026
                      xgrammar Cache
                      A caching mechanism in AI inference systems that stores grammar rules or parsing structures to improve performance.
                      FinServ context: High-frequency trading systems using vLLM for market analysis could be disrupted by attackers flooding the xgrammar cache, causing denial of service during critical trading periods.
                      First seen: 11 April 2026
                      XML-RPC (XML Remote Procedure Call)
                      A protocol that allows software running on different operating systems to make function calls across a network using XML to encode the data and HTTP to transport it.
                      FinServ context: In financial services web applications, XML-RPC endpoints can be exploited by attackers with low-level credentials to make unauthorised changes to system settings, as seen in WordPress-based customer-facing portals.
                      First seen: 24 June 2026
                      XOR-based Payloads
                      Malicious code that uses XOR encryption to obfuscate SQL injection attacks and evade security detection.
                      FinServ context: Attackers use these encrypted payloads to extract customer financial data from banking databases while avoiding fraud detection systems.
                      First seen: 10 April 2026
                      XPC Service
                      Apple's inter-process communication mechanism that allows different programs to securely exchange data and requests.
                      FinServ context: Unprotected XPC services in financial applications could allow attackers to escalate privileges and access sensitive trading algorithms or customer financial data.
                      First seen: 07 April 2026
                      Y
                      YellowKey / GreenPlasma / MiniPlasma
                      The security risk introduced when code written or suggested by an AI tool contains subtle flaws, insecure patterns, or vulnerabilities that a developer may accept without sufficient review.
                      FinServ context: FinTech firms adopting AI coding assistants to accelerate development must ensure security review processes are not bypassed, as AI-generated code at scale can introduce vulnerabilities across multiple systems simultaneously.
                      First seen: 26 May 2026
                      Z
                      Zero Trust
                      A security model that assumes no user or device should be automatically trusted, requiring continuous verification of identity and permissions before granting access to any resource.
                      FinServ context: UK financial regulators increasingly expect firms to adopt zero trust principles to limit the blast radius of compromised credentials or insider threats within banking networks.
                      First seen: 20 April 2026
                      Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA)
                      A security model that requires every user and device to be continuously verified before being granted access to specific applications or resources, regardless of whether they are inside or outside the corporate network.
                      FinServ context: UK financial institutions are adopting ZTNA to replace legacy VPN architectures and meet FCA operational resilience expectations by ensuring no implicit trust is granted to internal network traffic.
                      First seen: 27 May 2026
                      Zero-Day Vulnerability
                      A security flaw in software that is unknown to the vendor and has no available patch, meaning attackers can exploit it before any defence is possible.
                      FinServ context: Storm-1175 combined zero-day exploitation with high operational speed, meaning financial institutions cannot rely solely on patching cycles and must have detection controls that identify exploitation behaviour in real time.
                      First seen: 18 April 2026
